name: neurofeedback_adhd_consumer_device_clinic_claim_gap
premise: Clinic medical director Dr. Priya Raman must decide whether to allow staff to recommend a consumer neurofeedback headset whose randomized trial was withdrawn for incomplete blinding while parents demand non-pharm alternatives tonight. Device rep Carla Mendez cites improved parent-reported scores in gray literature. Banning referrals preserves evidence standards but sends families to influencers selling unregulated rigs. Permitting may imply institutional endorsement under FTC health claim scrutiny.

agents.name: Dr. Priya Raman
agents.identity: I am the clinic medical director accountable for evidence-based pediatrics, liability for device referrals, and parental autonomy in ADHD management plans.
agents.goal: I want families supported without laundering weak trials into white-coat endorsements kids will cite forever.

agents.name: Carla Mendez
agents.identity: I am the device account lead who can fund supervised home pilots if the clinic will co-brand a cautious protocol with us this quarter.
agents.goal: I want a pragmatic pilot pathway because stimulant shortages are real and kids are suffering tonight without appointments.

choices: I will ban formal referrals and publish a neutral handout comparing evidence tiers without naming brands, accepting families may still buy devices without guidance.
I will approve a tightly consented in-clinic supervised pilot with independent data custody, accepting opportunity cost and reputational tie to a vendor we may later abandon.
forced_choice.call_to_action: After-hours triage lines are jammed with ADHD medication refill failures. Dr. Priya Raman must choose between neutral handouts without referrals or a supervised vendor pilot. What does Dr. Priya Raman do?
